signal.description: A bit-field within a message and its raw→physical interpretation. Modeled on canspec-ui CanSignal, with kind/conversion consolidated and MDC display/alarm value-adds.

name.description: Signal name, unique within its message.

startBit.description: Start bit position. Numbering convention depends on `byteOrder`; see mdc-overview.md. Max 511 = bit 7 of byte 63 in a 64-byte CAN FD frame.

lengthBits.description: Width in bits. Floats must be 16, 32, or 64 (enforced by tooling).

multiplexer.description: Multiplexing role. Omit for plain always-present signals.

spn.description: SAE J1939 Suspect Parameter Number, if applicable.

invalid.description: Physical value that denotes "signal not available / invalid".

receivers.description: Nodes that consume this signal (DBC receivers).

computedSignal.description: A virtual signal derived from other signals via an expression rather than decoded from frame bits. Evaluated by the engine after decode. Has no startBit/lengthBits. Shares value semantics (unit/range/enumeration/display/alarms) with `signal` via `signalValueCommon`, including the `choices`/`valueTableRef` mutual exclusion.

name.description: Virtual signal name, unique within its scope.

expr.description: Expression over other signals' physical values, referenced by name (and optionally `message.signal` for cross-message). Grammar defined by the engine evaluator; see mdc-overview.md.

dependsOn.description: Explicit list of signals the expression reads, for dependency ordering and validation. Tools may also infer this from `expr`.
